title: syntherisma malabarica
intro: Syntherisma malabarica, also known as Malabar sida or Malabar mallow, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Ghats region in India. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Syntherisma malabarica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, making it a valuable resource in Ayurvedic and traditional medicine practices.
